what: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: emaxnls package provides tools nonlinear least squares estimation Emax regression models. supplies clean interface specifying Emax regression models covariates, using nls() underlying tool fitting model. supports least squares estimation using Gauss-Newton algorithm, Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm (via minpack.lm::nls.lm()) ‘nl2sol’ algorithm Port library. Continuous binary response variables supported, iterative reweighted least squares method used produce estimates binary case.

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models

code: install.packages("emaxnls") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("djnavarro/emaxnls")

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: Emax regression model continuous response variables estimated using emax_nls() function, using structural_model argument specify exposure variable response variable, covariate_model argument specify covariates estimated structural parameters (e.g., E0, Emax).

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: binary outcomes, package provides emax_logistic() function, estimates parameters logistic Emax model. model logit-link function binomial family assumed, parameters estimated using iterative reweighted nonlinear least squares procedure.
code: # estimate parameters for a logistic Emax regression with covariates mod_b <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1, # specify the response and exposure variables   covariate_model = list(     E0 ~ cnt_a,  # add a covariate on the E0 intercept parameter     Emax ~ 1,    # no covariates on Emax     logEC50 ~ 1  # no covariates on logEC50   ),    data = emax_df )  mod_b #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:       exp_1  #>   Response:       rsp_2  #>   Emax type:      hyperbolic  #>   Response type:  binary (logit link) #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:  400  #>   Residual df:   396  #>   Deviance:      331.4698  #>   AIC:           339.4698  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error  lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.659    0.0800  0.501  0.816 #> 2 E0_Intercept        -5.00     0.578  -6.14  -3.87  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       8.12     2.27    5.08  17.6   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    9.78     0.518   8.89  11.0   #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.  # hypothesis tests produced by summary() summary(mod_b) #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   label             estimate std_error z_statistic   p_value ci_lower ci_upper #>   <chr>                <dbl>     <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.659    0.0800        8.24  1.79e-16    0.501    0.816 #> 2 E0_Intercept        -5.00     0.578        -8.64  5.43e-18   -6.14    -3.87  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       8.12     2.27          3.58  3.45e- 4    5.08    17.6   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    9.78     0.518        NA    NA           8.89    11.0

title: Nonlinear Least Squares Estimation for Emax Regression Models
text: package supports stepwise covariate modeling via forward addition backward elimination. emax_scm_forward() function supports forward addition, emax_scm_backward() function supports backward elimination, syntax designed allow forward-backward procedures piping base model emax_scm_forward() emax_scm_backward().
code: # base model with no covariates base_model <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )  # list of possible consider for E0 and Emax covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  # stepwise covariate modeling with a forward step and a backward step final_mod <- base_model |>    emax_scm_forward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .01) |>    emax_scm_backward(candidates = covariate_list, threshold = .001)   # extract the complete history of all models tested during the  # stepwise covariate modeling procedure emax_scm_history(final_mod) #> # A tibble: 22 × 11 #>    iteration attempt step       action term_tested  model_tested model_converged #>        <int>   <int> <chr>      <chr>  <chr>        <chr>        <lgl>           #>  1         0       0 base model <NA>   <NA>         E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  2         1       1 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_c   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  3         1       2 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_e E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  4         1       3 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_b   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  5         1       4 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_c E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  6         1       5 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_a E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  7         1       6 forward    add    Emax ~ bin_d E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #>  8         1       7 forward    add    E0 ~ cnt_a   E0 ~ 1 + cn… TRUE            #>  9         1       8 forward    add    Emax ~ cnt_b E0 ~ 1, Ema… TRUE            #> 10         1       9 forward    add    E0 ~ bin_e   E0 ~ 1 + bi… TRUE            #> # ℹ 12 more rows #> # ℹ 4 more variables: term_p_value <dbl>, model_aic <dbl>, model_bic <dbl>, #> #   model_updated <lgl>  # show the final model final_mod #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.

title: Akaike information criterion / Bayesian information criterion — AIC.emaxlogistic
text: AIC applies penalty 2 * k minus twice log-likelihood, k number estimated parameters. BIC applies log(n) * k, making conservative AIC large samples. multiple models passed, non-converging models dropped warning.

title: Akaike information criterion / Bayesian information criterion — AIC.emaxlogistic
code: mod_0 <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) mod_1 <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df )  # calculate AIC for individual models AIC(mod_0) #> [1] 1281.131 AIC(mod_1) #> [1] 603.6431  # calculate AIC for a sequence of models AIC(mod_0, mod_1) #>       df       AIC #> mod_0  4 1281.1314 #> mod_1  5  603.6431  # calculate BIC for individual models BIC(mod_0) #> [1] 1297.097 BIC(mod_1) #> [1] 623.6004  # calculate BIC for a sequence of models BIC(mod_0, mod_1) #>       df       BIC #> mod_0  4 1297.0973 #> mod_1  5  623.6004  # emaxlogistic models mod_b0 <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) mod_b1 <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) AIC(mod_b0, mod_b1) #>        df      AIC #> mod_b0  3 444.4336 #> mod_b1  4 339.4698 BIC(mod_b0, mod_b1) #>        df      BIC #> mod_b0  3 456.4080 #> mod_b1  4 355.4356

title: Analysis of variance for Emax regression models — anova.emaxlogistic
text: emaxnls objects, calls stats::anova() underlying nls objects produce ANOVA table sequence models. emaxlogistic objects, computes likelihood ratio chi-squared test comparing nested models; test statistic difference deviances reference distribution chi-squared degrees freedom equal difference number parameters. nesting assumption checked; results interpretable successive model genuinely adds parameters previous one.

title: Analysis of variance for Emax regression models — anova.emaxlogistic
code: mod_0 <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) mod_1 <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) anova(mod_0, mod_1) #> Analysis of Variance Table #>  #> Model 1: rsp_1 ~ ((1 * E0_Intercept)) + exp_1 * ((1 * Emax_Intercept))/(exp_1 + exp((1 * logEC50_Intercept))) #> Model 2: rsp_1 ~ ((cnt_a * E0_cnt_a) + (1 * E0_Intercept)) + exp_1 * ((1 * Emax_Intercept))/(exp_1 + exp((1 * logEC50_Intercept))) #>   Res.Df Res.Sum Sq Df Sum Sq F value    Pr(>F)     #> 1    397     564.77                                 #> 2    396     103.31  1 461.46  1768.9 < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1  # emaxlogistic: likelihood ratio test mod_b0 <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) mod_b1 <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df ) anova(mod_b0, mod_b1) #>      Df Deviance Df_diff    LRT  Pr(>Chi)     #> [1,]  3   438.43                              #> [2,]  4   331.47       1 106.96 < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
text: function arguments param data evaluates Emax model supplied (default) parameter values data. emaxnls objects return values response scale; emaxlogistic objects predicted probabilities \((0, 1)\).

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
text: extracted function accepts data param arguments. default values used fitting model. supplying custom values, data must contain variables used model, param must named numeric vector whose names exactly match returned coef(mod). Scale predictions. emaxnls objects returned function produces predictions response scale (scale outcome variable). emaxlogistic objects structural Emax model parameterized logit scale — logit(p) = E0 + Emax * x / (x + EC50) — emax_fun() applies inverse-logit transformation returning, predictions probability scale. consistent default behavior fitted() predict() emaxlogistic objects. need linear predictor (logit scale) directly, use fitted(object, type = "link") predict(object, type = "link").

title: Construct Emax prediction function from model object — emax_fun
code: mod_c <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )  if (emax_converged(mod_c)) {    par <- coef(mod_c)   mod_fn <- emax_fun(mod_c)      # apply the function to a few rows of the original data   mod_fn(data = emax_df[120:125, ], param = par)      # adjust the parameters and re-evaluate   new_par <- par   new_par["E0_Intercept"] <- 0   mod_fn(data = emax_df[120:125, ], param = new_par)  } #> [1]  9.595929  3.412750  8.208382 10.674149  1.200782  8.567196  # for emaxlogistic, the returned function gives probabilities mod_b <- emax_logistic(   structural_model = rsp_2 ~ exp_1,   covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),   data = emax_df )  if (emax_converged(mod_b)) {   mod_fn_b <- emax_fun(mod_b)   mod_fn_b(data = emax_df[120:125, ]) } #> [1] 0.73738728 0.40712302 0.38375750 0.92637415 0.03314224 0.39944030

title: Estimate parameters for a logistic Emax regression model — emax_logistic
text: structural Emax model placed log-odds (logit) scale: logit(p) = E0 + Emax * x / (x + EC50)  (hyperbolic) logit(p) = E0 + Emax * x^h / (x^h + EC50^h)  (sigmoidal) Estimation uses iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS). outer iteration weighted NLS problem solved using working weights working response derived current parameter estimates. equivalent Fisher scoring produces maximum likelihood estimates convergence. interface mirrors emax_nls() function continuous response models: structural_model covariate_model arguments specification, including support sigmoidal models via logHill term. response variable structural_model must binary (0/1) numeric vector.

title: Estimate parameters for an Emax regression model — emax_nls
text: Pass two-sided formula structural_model specify response exposure variables (e.g., response ~ exposure), list formulas covariate_model specify covariates. minimum covariate model requires formulas E0, Emax, logEC50. formula like E0 ~ age + group includes age group covariates baseline response; use Emax ~ 1 covariates added parameter. fit sigmoidal Emax model (estimating Hill parameter), include formula logHill covariate_model, e.g. logHill ~ 1. Without term hyperbolic model fitted. Interaction terms covariate model currently supported. Starting values constructed automatically via emax_nls_init() unless init argument supplied manually. Three optimization algorithms available; see emax_nls_options() details.

code: # use a heuristic to construct sensible start values, and plausible # upper and lower bounds within which the estimate is expected to fall  emax_nls_init(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   parameter covariate start  lower upper #>   <chr>     <chr>     <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 E0        cnt_a      0    -7.84   7.84 #> 2 E0        Intercept  9.73  0.528 18.9  #> 3 Emax      Intercept  7.74 -1.47  16.9  #> 4 logEC50   Intercept  8.69  6.63  10.8   # compare to the values estimated: coef(emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ cnt_a, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )) #>          E0_cnt_a      E0_Intercept    Emax_Intercept logEC50_Intercept  #>         0.4861467         5.0548076         9.9697250         8.2688405

title: Settings used to estimate Emax model — emax_nls_options
text: present three supported values optim_method: "gauss": Estimate parameters using Gauss-Newton algorithm. equivalent using "default" option nls() "port": Estimate parameters using bounded optimization "nl2sol" algorithm Port library. Equivalent "port" nls() "levenberg": Estimate parameters using Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm. equivalent using nlsLM() "minpack.lm" package. Note Golub-Pereyra algorithm partially linear least-squares (.e. "plinear" option nls()) currently supported Emax regression. Informal testing suggests perform well models, rarely converges. optim_control argument mirrors corresponding control arguments respective optimization methods: "gauss" "port": list match output stats::nls.control() "levenberg": list match output minpack.lm::nls.lm.control() optim_control = NULL, default settings used relevant function.

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: Performs stepwise covariate modeling forward addition (emax_scm_forward()), backward elimination (emax_scm_backward()), sequence. Use emax_scm_history() retrieve history models tested procedure.

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: emax_scm_forward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.01, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_backward(mod, candidates, threshold = 0.001, seed = NULL)  emax_scm_history(mod)

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: mod emaxnls object candidates list candidate covariates threshold Threshold addition removal seed Seed RNG state

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: object class emaxnls

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
text: candidates argument must named list whose names correspond structural parameters (e.g. E0, Emax) whose values character vectors covariate names consider. See examples illustration. present, covariate selection uses p-values criterion: term added p-value falls threshold (forward) removed p-value exceeds threshold (backward). Selection AIC criteria may supported future. seed argument controls RNG state stochastic components procedure. currently experimental may removed future releases. Every model tested procedure stored internally returned object. Use emax_scm_history() extract record.

title: Stepwise covariate modeling for Emax regression — emax_scm
code: base_model <- emax_nls(   structural_model = rsp_1 ~ exp_1,    covariate_model = list(E0 ~ 1, Emax ~ 1, logEC50 ~ 1),    data = emax_df )  covariate_list <- list(   E0 = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e"),   Emax = c("cnt_a", "cnt_b", "cnt_c", "bin_d", "bin_e") )  # add covariates to the base model using forward addition forward_model <- emax_scm_forward(   mod = base_model,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .01 ) forward_model #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.  # remove covariates from the forward model using backward deletion final_model <- emax_scm_backward(   mod = forward_model,   candidates = covariate_list,    threshold = .001 )  final_model #> Structural model: #>  #>   Exposure:  exp_1  #>   Response:  rsp_1  #>   Emax type: hyperbolic  #>  #>   Response type:  continuous #>  #> Covariate model: #>  #>   E0:       E0 ~ 1 + cnt_a  #>   Emax:     Emax ~ 1  #>   logEC50:  logEC50 ~ 1  #>  #> Model fit: #>  #>   Observations:         400  #>   Residual df:          396  #>   Residual std. error:  0.5108  #>   AIC:                  603.6431  #>  #> Coefficients (95% CI): #>  #>   label             estimate std_error lower  upper #> 1 E0_cnt_a             0.486    0.0116 0.463  0.509 #> 2 E0_Intercept         5.05     0.0759 4.91   5.20  #> 3 Emax_Intercept       9.97     0.112  9.75  10.2   #> 4 logEC50_Intercept    8.27     0.0394 8.19   8.35  #>  #> Use summary() for hypothesis tests.  # what: Which tests are reported by default
title: Summary of an Emax regression model — summary.emaxlogistic
text: parameters meaningful point null zero: Emax_Intercept: tests whether exposure-response relationship exists. logHill_Intercept: tests whether logHill = 0, .e., whether Hill parameter equals 1 concentration scale, mean sigmoidal model reduces hyperbolic one. E0_Intercept: tests whether baseline response zero. informative outcome expressed change baseline, common convention pharmacometrics. Covariate beta terms: test whether given covariate effect corresponding structural parameter. one exception logEC50_Intercept. model parameterized terms logEC50 (log-concentration scale), EC50 directly, null H0: logEC50 = 0 corresponds testing EC50 = 1 concentration scale — value intrinsic pharmacometric meaning. default, test statistic p-value logEC50_Intercept suppressed (set NA), confidence interval logEC50 retained. work EC50 concentration scale, use back_transform = TRUE.

title: Summary of an Emax regression model — summary.emaxlogistic
text: simultaneous = TRUE, single critical value derived joint multivariate normal distribution standardized parameter estimates, using correlation matrix implied vcov(). resulting intervals simultaneous coverage conf_level wider individual (pointwise) intervals.

title: emaxnls 0.1.1.9000
text: Adds emax_logistic() fitting binary-outcome Emax models using iterative reweighted least squares (IRLS), along emax_logistic_init() emax_logistic_options() initialization configuration. standard S3 methods (coef(), vcov(), confint(), residuals(), fitted(), predict(), anova(), logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), deviance(), simulate()) supported new emaxlogistic class. Redesigns print() summary() methods emaxnls emaxlogistic objects: print() now concise model overview showing structural covariate formulas, fit statistics (observations, residual df, sigma deviance, AIC), coefficient table estimates, standard errors, confidence intervals. Hypothesis tests shown print(); footer line directs users summary(). conf_level argument controls interval level (default 0.95). summary() gains three new arguments: suppress_nonsensical = TRUE: suppresses test statistic p-value logEC50_Intercept default. logEC50 intercept estimated log-concentration scale, testing H0: logEC50 = 0 corresponds testing EC50 = 1 concentration scale — threshold pharmacometric meaning. confidence interval logEC50 always reported. Pass suppress_nonsensical = FALSE restore raw test. p_adjust = "none": adjusts non-NA p-values via p.adjust() multiple-comparison correction; suppressed p-values excluded adjustment set. simultaneous = FALSE: TRUE, computes simultaneous Wald confidence intervals using mvtnorm::qmvnorm() correlation matrix vcov(), giving intervals joint coverage conf_level across parameters simultaneously. summary(back_transform = TRUE) now also sets test statistic NA back-transformed parameters, consistent standard error already NA back-transformed scale. confint() now falls back Wald intervals warning profile likelihood computation fails (can occur sigmoidal models).

title: emaxnls 0.1.1.9000
text: Fixes AIC() BIC() called multiple model arguments (#37). Fixes na.action parameter passed correctly emax_nls() emax_logistic() (#38). Fixes predict() omitting residual.scale return value se.fit = TRUE (#39). Fixes crashes emax_logistic_init() prevents Inf parameter bounds arising initialisation (#40). Hardens .nls_call() avoid cryptic errors optimisation fails, tightens argument validation emax_fun() (#41). Adds input validation binary response variable emax_logistic(), informative errors non-binary --range values (#42). Fixes validator error messages reference public API parameter names rather internal names (#43). Fixes NaN produced .binomial_deviance() predicted probabilities exactly 0 1 (boundary cases) (#44).

title: emaxnls 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-30 Expanded description package. Fixes bug emax_nls_init() called manually. anova() now warns rather errors fewer two converged models supplied. Additional examples documentation. Improved unit tests.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN submission. package provides tools fitting analysing Emax dose-response models via nonlinear least squares.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_nls() fits continuous-response Emax models using NLS, supporting hyperbolic (E0 + Emax * x / (EC50 + x)) sigmoidal (E0 + Emax * x^Hill / (EC50^Hill + x^Hill)) model forms. emax_nls_options() configures optimisation algorithm control parameters. Three algorithms supported via optim_method argument: "gauss" (Gauss-Newton, default), "port" (bounded nl2sol), "levenberg" (Levenberg-Marquardt via minpack.lm). emax_nls_init() generates starting values parameter bounds automatically data, including support categorical covariates. Users can also call directly inspect override initialisation fitting.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: Covariates can added structural parameter (E0, Emax, logEC50, logHill) via formula interface, e.g. emax_nls(rsp ~ dose, data = df, E0 = ~ group + age). emax_add_term() emax_remove_term() update fitted model adding removing single covariate term without refitting scratch.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_scm_forward() performs forward covariate selection, adding one term time likelihood-ratio test p-value stays threshold. emax_scm_backward() performs backward elimination, removing terms p-value exceeds threshold. emax_scm_history() returns sequence models tested SCM procedure, including AIC, BIC, convergence status candidate.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: print() displays brief model summary. summary() produces coefficient table standard errors, confidence intervals, hypothesis tests. back_transform = TRUE argument re-expresses log-scaled parameters (logEC50, logHill) natural scales (EC50, Hill) output. coef() extracts parameter vector; supports back_transform = TRUE. vcov() returns estimated variance-covariance matrix. confint() computes profile-likelihood confidence intervals. residuals() fitted() return model residuals fitted values. predict() generates predictions, optionally standard errors. anova() compares nested models likelihood-ratio test. logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), sigma(), nobs(), df.residual() return standard model-fit statistics. simulate() draws Monte Carlo replicates resampling parameters asymptotic normal distribution estimates (requires mvtnorm). emax_fun() extracts standalone prediction function fitted model can evaluated arbitrary dose values parameter vectors. emax_converged() returns TRUE FALSE indicating whether optimiser converged. S3 methods handle non-convergent models gracefully returning emaxnls_null object rather erroring.

title: emaxnls 0.1.0
text: emax_df: synthetic dataset 400 observations across four dose groups (0, 100, 200, 300), continuous response, binary response, two exposure metrics, continuous binary covariates, categorical covariate.
